description: IPC granted to the live web app over HTTPS. The remote page is treated as semi-trusted (assume MITM/compromise risk), so it is granted ONLY the non-privileged runtime-info command, the sign-video-challenge attestation command, the update/reload controls, and the core baseline needed for IPC. sign-video-challenge is a DELIBERATE remote grant: it returns only an HMAC signature over a server-issued nonce (single-use, server-verified), so the page can never exfiltrate the build-time secret or mint reusable credentials. updater-check/updater-install/app-relaunch and hard-reload are also DELIBERATE grants for the in-app update UI + Reload button: the updater only pulls minisign-verified artifacts from the configured endpoint (a malicious page cannot substitute an unsigned payload), app-relaunch merely restarts the app, and hard-reload only clears the WebView cache and re-navigates to this same trusted origin — none exfiltrate data or widen the trust boundary. The privileged commands (OS keyring secrets, offline cache, speaking-audio temp files, dropped-file probing, notifications, open-external) are deliberately NOT granted to the remote origin — they stay registered for localhost/dev and future native re-enablement. Re-add the matching allow-* here only if a feature is moved to native desktop capture.
